title: Speech or language disturbances
Description: Speech or language disturbances, not classified elsewhere include dysphasia and aphasia, dysarthria and anarthria, and other speech or language disturbances.
Exclusions: Developmental speech or language disorders; Autism spectrum disorder; Speech dysfluency

title: Aphasia
Exclusions: Developmental speech or language disorders
Index Terms: Aphasia; aphasic; aphasic disturbance; Conduction aphasia; Central aphasia; Amnestic aphasia; nominal aphasia; Global aphasia; Cortical aphasia; Expressive-receptive aphasia; Paraphasia; Pictorial aphasia; Semantic aphasia; Syntactic aphasia; Syntactical aphasia

title: Dysphasia
Description: A cognitive disorder marked by an impaired ability to comprehend or express language in its written or spoken form. This condition is caused by diseases which affect the language areas of the dominant hemisphere. Clinical features are used to classify the various subtypes of this condition.
Exclusions: Developmental speech or language disorders; progressive isolated aphasia
Index Terms: Dysphasia; loss of power of expression or comprehension; Alalia; Aphemia; Semantic dysphasia; syntactic difficulties; syntactic impairment; Dysnomia; Global dysphasia; Expressive dysphasia

title: Dysarthria
Exclusions: Developmental speech or language disorders

title: Anarthria
Index Terms: Anarthria

title: Speech dysfluency
Description: Speech dysfluency is characterised by the frequent or pervasive disruption of the rhythmic flow of speech that arises subsequent to the developmental period (i.e., adult onset) and is outside the limits of normal variation and results in reduced intelligibility and significantly affects communication. It can involve repetitions of sounds, syllables or words, prolongations, word breaks, blockage of production, excessive use of interjections, and rapid short bursts of speech.
Exclusions: Developmental language disorder; Developmental speech or language disorders; Developmental speech fluency disorder; Dysarthria; Selective mutism; childhood onset stammering; childhood onset stuttering; childhood onset cluttering; childhood-onset speech fluency disorder
Index Terms: Speech dysfluency; speech impediment NOS; Adult onset cluttering; Adult onset stammering; Adult onset stuttering

title: Voice disturbances
Description: Voice disturbances include dysphonia, aphonia, hypernasality and hyponasality, and other voice disturbances.

title: Aphonia
Description: Aphonia is the inability to produce voice. It is considered more severe than dysphonia. Like dysphonia, aphonia can be caused by voice strain or overuse, injury, by structural laryngeal anomalies or by dystonic neurological disorders.
Exclusions: Dissociative disorders
Index Terms: Aphonia; Loss of voice; absence of voice; complete voice loss; organic aphonia; unable to phonate; unable to produce voice; unable to vocalize

title: Dysphonia
Description: Difficulty and/or pain in phonation or speaking.
Exclusions: Developmental speech or language disorders; Developmental speech fluency disorder

title: Hoarseness
Index Terms: Hoarseness

title: Nasality
Description: Nasality (or resonance) refers to the quality of the voice that is determined by the balance of sound vibration in the oral, nasal, and pharyngeal cavities during speech. Abnormal resonance can occur when there is obstruction in one of the cavities, causing hyponasality, or when there is velopharyngeal dysfunction, causing hypernasality. This category should only be assigned when hyponasality or hypernasality is outside the limits of normal variation and results in reduced intelligibility and significantly affects communication.
Index Terms: Nasality; Hypernasality; Hyponasality

title: Symptoms, signs or clinical findings involving consciousness
Description: Symptoms, signs, and clinical findings indicative of a disturbance in the state or quality of awareness of oneself and the environment, alertness, or clarity of the wakeful state.
Exclusions: newborn uremic coma; Delirium; Psychomotor retardation
Index Terms: Symptoms, signs or clinical findings involving consciousness

title: Stupor
Description: Total or nearly total lack of spontaneous movement and marked decrease in reactivity to environment.
Exclusions: Catatonia; Delirium
Index Terms: Stupor; Semicoma; stuporous

title: Coma
Description: Acute state lasting more than one hour and usually less than a month. The comatose patient is unresponsive, lying with their eyes closed and cannot be aroused even by vigorous and noxious stimuli. Motor responses to noxious stimulation are limited to reflexive behaviour. Etiologies include but are not limited to traumatic, anoxic, infectious, neoplastic, vascular, inflammatory and metabolic brain injuries.
Exclusions: Diabetic coma; Hepatic coma; Neonatal coma; Nondiabetic hypoglycaemic coma; chronic uremic coma

title: Clouding of consciousness
Description: An impairment in the clarity of consciousness characterised by impaired ability to comprehend aspects of the environment or the self in relation to the environment, inattention, and abnormalities in thought processes, comprehension. It is typically accompanied by subjective experience of mental clouding described as feeling ‘foggy’. Clouding of consciousness is a common form of cognitive disturbance in Delirium, but it is not synonymous with Delirium because Delirium includes additional diagnostic requirements.
Exclusions: Delirium
Index Terms: Clouding of consciousness; Clouded state; brain fog; mental fog; Subsyndromal delirium

title: Symptoms, signs or clinical findings involving cognition
Description: Symptoms, signs, and clinical findings indicative of a disturbance in mental abilities and processes related to attention, memory, judgment, reasoning, problem solving, decision making, or comprehension, or the integration of these functions.

title: Age-associated cognitive decline
Description: A normative (non-pathological) deterioration of higher cortical functions such as thinking, reasoning, comprehension, calculation, learning, language, and judgment.
Index Terms: Age-associated cognitive decline; Age related cognitive decline; Age-associated memory impairment; Ageing-related cognitive decline; Senile degeneration of brain, not elsewhere classified; senile brain degeneration; senile degeneration of brain

title: Amnesia
Description: An inability to recall past experiences, especially where recall is to be expected.
Exclusions: Dissociative disorders

title: Anterograde amnesia
Description: An inability to recall past experiences, especially where recall is to be expected, occurring after an event (psychological or physical) presumed to be responsible for the amnesia.
Index Terms: Anterograde amnesia; antegrade amnesia

title: Retrograde amnesia
Description: An inability to recall past experiences, especially where recall is to be expected, preceding an event (psychological or physical) presumed to be responsible for the amnesia.
Index Terms: Retrograde amnesia

title: Transient global amnesia
Description: A time-limited episode (lasting up to two days) of short-term memory loss without other signs or symptoms of neurological impairment.
Index Terms: Transient global amnesia; Transient global amnesia, course of resolution unspecified; Transient global amnesia, complete resolution within 1 to 24 hours; Transient global amnesia, complete resolution within less than 1 hour

title: Anosognosia
Description: A lack of awareness or failure to recognize one's own illness, symptoms, or functional deficits, considered to be an aspect of the illness.
Index Terms: Anosognosia

title: Confabulation
Description: The filling of memory gaps with fabricated, distorted, or misinterpreted memories about oneself or the world, without the conscious intention to deceive.
Index Terms: Confabulation

title: Disorientation
Description: Impairment in or loss of awareness of the position of the self in relation to place, time, situation, or other persons. In severe cases, the sense of personal identity may also be lost.
Index Terms: Disorientation; mental confusion NOS

title: Distractibility
Description: Difficulty focusing on tasks; attention is easily diverted by extraneous stimuli.
Index Terms: Distractibility

title: Impaired abstract thinking
Description: The inability to use concepts and to make and understand generalizations, such as the identifying the properties or pattern shared by a variety of specific items or events.
Index Terms: Impaired abstract thinking

title: Impaired executive functioning
Description: Impairment in higher-level cognitive abilities, such as planning, sequencing, concept formation, abstracting, and decision-making.
Index Terms: Impaired executive functioning

title: Impaired judgment
Description: Deficit in the capacity to make sound, reasoned, and responsible decisions.
Index Terms: Impaired judgment

title: Perseveration
Description: Persistent repetition of previously used words, phrases, or details that are not responsive to the demands of the situation.
Index Terms: Perseveration

title: Poor concentration
Description: Difficulty focusing attention and sustaining the mental energy necessary to accomplish a task or goal.
Index Terms: Poor concentration

title: Racing thoughts
Description: Subjective perception of accelerated thought processes.
Index Terms: Racing thoughts

title: Symptoms or signs involving motivation or energy
Description: Symptoms and signs involving motivation (the process that initiates, guides, and maintains goal-oriented behaviours) or energy (the strength and vitality required for sustained physical or mental activity).

title: Avolition
Description: A general lack of drive, or lack of motivation to pursue meaningful goals (e.g., as evidenced by limited participation in work, school, or socializing with others).
Index Terms: Avolition

title: Decreased libido
Description: Decreased sexual desire or sexual activity compared with the patient's usual levels of sexual interest and functioning.
Index Terms: Decreased libido

title: Demoralization
Description: Loss of confidence in one's ability to cope, with associated feelings of helplessness, hopelessness, and discouragement.
Index Terms: Demoralization

title: Hopelessness
Description: Little or no belief in a positive future.
Index Terms: Hopelessness

title: Increased energy
Description: Increased physical or mental resources for activity, typically characterised by increased capacity for work and greater efficiency in responding to stimuli.
Index Terms: Increased energy

title: Increased goal-directed activity
Description: Increased planning of and participation in multiple activities (e.g. sexual, occupational, political, religious), compared to the individual's typical level of activity.
Index Terms: Increased goal-directed activity; behaviour of overactivity; overactivity

title: Increased libido
Description: Increased sexual desire or sexual activity compared with the patient's usual levels of sexual interest and functioning.
Index Terms: Increased libido

title: Tiredness
Description: Feeling of reduced alertness and an accompanying decrease in mental acuity, in some cases resulting in an impulse or tendency to fall asleep.
Index Terms: Tiredness

title: Symptoms or signs involving appearance or behaviour
Exclusions: Insufficient intake of food or water due to self neglect

title: Aggressive behaviour
Description: Actions intended to threaten or hurt another person or to damage property that may be physical, verbal, or symbolic (e.g., acting against the other person's interests). Aggressive behaviour may be appropriate and self-protective, or inappropriate, hostile, and destructive.
Index Terms: Aggressive behaviour; Violent behaviour; physical violence

title: Antisocial behaviour
Description: Behaviour in which the basic rights of others or major age-appropriate societal norms, rules, or laws, are violated.
Index Terms: Antisocial behaviour; Child or adolescent antisocial behaviour

title: Avoidance behaviour
Description: The act of keeping away from circumstances, situations, or stimuli that cause anxiety or other negative emotions in the individual.
Index Terms: Avoidance behaviour

title: Bradyphrenia
Description: Slowness of thoughts or fatigability of initiative
Index Terms: Bradyphrenia

title: Compulsions
Description: Repetitive behaviours or rituals (e.g., washing, checking) or mental acts (e.g., repeating words silently) that the individual feels driven to perform in response to an obsession, according to rigid rules, or to achieve a sense of ‘completeness’.
Exclusions: Obsessive-compulsive disorder
Index Terms: Compulsions; obsessive compulsive behaviour

title: Coprolalia
Description: Involuntary swearing or the involuntary utterance of obscene words or socially inappropriate and derogatory remarks, often in Tourette syndrome.
Exclusions: Tourette syndrome
Index Terms: Coprolalia

title: Disorganised behaviour
Description: Behaviour including posture, gait, and other activity that is unpredictable or not goal-directed (e.g., shouting at strangers on the street).
Index Terms: Disorganised behaviour; inexplicable behaviour; strange behaviour

title: Disheveled appearance
Description: Untidy or unkempt appearance reflecting a lack of attention to one or more aspects of hygiene, grooming, or dress.
Index Terms: Disheveled appearance

title: Disruptive behaviour
Description: Behaviour that causes disorder and turmoil in others or one's environment (e.g., angry outbursts, arguments, disobedience).
Exclusions: Disruptive behaviour or dissocial disorders
Index Terms: Disruptive behaviour

title: Echolalia
Description: The automatic repetition of vocalizations, words, or phrases uttered by another person, which may be immediate or delayed (e.g., repetition of phrases earlier heard on television), without meaningful communicative function. Echolalia is a common feature of communication abnormalities in Autism spectrum disorder, but may also occur in other Mental, behavioural or neurodevelopmental disorders and certain neurological conditions, among children with severe visual impairment, and occasionally in typically developing children. Echolalia does not include repetition as a normal feature of language acquisition in early childhood development.
Exclusions: Autism spectrum disorder; Developmental language disorder
Index Terms: Echolalia

title: Excessive crying of child, adolescent, or adult
Description: Episodes of crying for several hours a day for more than several days a week for several weeks in an otherwise healthy child, adolescent, or adult.
Index Terms: Excessive crying of child, adolescent, or adult

title: Increased sociability
Description: Decrease or loss of normal social inhibitions manifested in increased impulses to be with and talk to other people, including overfamiliarity, compared to the individual's typical level of activity.
Index Terms: Increased sociability

title: Mutism
Description: A lack of verbal output that may be generalised or restricted to specific situations.
Index Terms: Mutism; muteness

title: Non-suicidal self-injury
Description: Intentional self-inflicted injury to the body, most commonly cutting, scraping, burning, biting, or hitting, with the expectation that the injury will lead to only minor physical harm.
Index Terms: Non-suicidal self-injury

title: Odd or peculiar appearance
Description: Grooming, clothing, or other aspects of personal appearance that are eccentric, unusual, or peculiar, and inconsistent with cultural or subcultural norms.
Index Terms: Odd or peculiar appearance; bizarre appearance; bizarre personal appearance; inappropriate appearance; odd appearance

title: Odd or peculiar behaviour
Description: Behaviour including posture and gait that is eccentric, unusual, or peculiar, and is inconsistent with cultural or subcultural norms.
Index Terms: Odd or peculiar behaviour

title: Panic attack
Description: A discrete episode of intense fear or apprehension accompanied by the rapid and concurrent onset of a number of characteristic symptoms. These symptoms may include, but are not limited to, palpitations or increased heart rate, sweating, trembling, sensations of shortness of breath, feelings of choking, chest pain, nausea or abdominal distress, feelings of dizziness or lightheadedness, chills or hot flushes, tingling or lack of sensation in extremities (i.e., paresthesias), depersonalization or derealization, fear of losing control or going mad, and fear of imminent death. Panic attacks can appear out of the blue or can be triggered by particular situations.
Exclusions: recurrent panic attacks; Panic disorder
Index Terms: Panic attack

title: Poor personal hygiene
Description: Unwillingness or inability to maintain a level of personal cleanliness that is in keeping with the standards of the person's culture, society, or setting, such as not washing or brushing one's teeth.
Index Terms: Poor personal hygiene; Very low level of personal hygiene

title: Poverty of speech
Description: A general lack of the unprompted content and elaboration normally seen in speech that is attributed to poverty of thought. It is one of the negative symptoms of Schizophrenia.
Index Terms: Poverty of speech; Alogia; Paucity of speech

title: Pressured speech
Description: Speech in which the person feels undue pressure to get the words out. The person’s speech is usually rapid, loud, and emphatic and may be difficult or impossible to interrupt. Frequently, the person talks without any social stimulation and may continue to talk even though no one is listening.
Exclusions: Schizophrenia or other primary psychotic disorders; Bipolar or related disorders
Index Terms: Pressured speech

title: Psychomotor agitation
Description: Excessive motor activity, usually manifested by purposeless behaviours such as fidgeting, shifting, fiddling, inability to sit or stand still, wringing of the hands, etc.
Index Terms: Psychomotor agitation

title: Psychomotor retardation
Description: A visible generalised slowing of movements and speech.
Exclusions: Stupor
Index Terms: Psychomotor retardation; poor responsiveness behaviour; slow behaviour; slowness and poor responsiveness

title: Social withdrawal
Description: Retreat from relationships and other social interactions
Index Terms: Social withdrawal

title: Suicide attempt
Description: A specific episode of self-harming behaviour undertaken with the conscious intention of ending one's life.
Index Terms: Suicide attempt

title: Suicidal behaviour
Description: Concrete actions, such as buying a gun or stockpiling medication, that are taken in preparation for fulfilling a wish to end one's life but that do not constitute an actual suicide attempt.
Index Terms: Suicidal behaviour

title: Symptoms or signs involving mood or affect
Description: Symptoms and signs involving the regulation and expression of emotions or feeling states.

title: Ambivalence
Description: Conflicting ideas, wishes, or feelings toward a person, thing or situation that are distressing and may create difficulties in making decisions.
Index Terms: Ambivalence

title: Anger
Description: An emotional state related to one's psychological interpretation of having been threatened that may range in intensity from mild irritation to intense fury and rage.
Index Terms: Anger

title: Anhedonia
Description: Inability to experience pleasure from normally pleasurable activities.
Index Terms: Anhedonia

title: Anxiety
Description: Apprehensiveness or anticipation of future danger or misfortune accompanied by a feeling of worry, distress, or somatic symptoms of tension. The focus of anticipated danger may be internal or external.
Index Terms: Anxiety; Nervous tension; Nervousness; nervous; nervous irritation; nervous trouble

title: Apathy
Description: A reduction or lack of feeling, emotion, interest, or concern; a state of indifference.
Index Terms: Apathy

title: Depressed mood
Description: Negative affective state characterised by low mood, sadness, emptiness, hopelessness, or dejection
Exclusions: Mood disorders; Low self-esteem
Index Terms: Depressed mood; Feeling depressed

title: Disturbance of affect
Description: A disturbance in the expression or outward manifestation of mood.

title: Constricted affect
Description: A marked reduction in the expressive range and intensity of affect, but less than is observed in Blunted affect.
Index Terms: Constricted affect

title: Blunted affect
Description: A severe reduction in the expressive range and intensity of affect, but less than is observed in Flat affect.
Index Terms: Blunted affect

title: Flat affect
Description: Absence or near absence of any sign of affective expression.
Index Terms: Flat affect

title: Labile affect
Description: Marked variability in emotional expression, with repeated, rapid, and abrupt shifts.
Index Terms: Labile affect

title: Inappropriate affect
Description: Affective expression that is discordant with the content of the person's speech or ideation, or incompatible with the demands of a particular situation.
Index Terms: Inappropriate affect

title: Dysphoria
Description: An unpleasant mood state, which can include feelings of depression, anxiety, discontent, irritability, and unhappiness
Index Terms: Dysphoria

title: Elevated mood
Description: A positive mood state typically characterised by increased energy and self-esteem which may be out of proportion to the individual's life circumstances.
Index Terms: Elevated mood

title: Euphoria
Description: An exaggerated feeling of physical and emotional well-being and vitality.
Index Terms: Euphoria; euphoric mood

title: Fear
Description: An emotional response to perceived imminent threat or danger associated with urges to flee or fight.
Index Terms: Fear

title: Feelings of guilt
Description: Remorse related to past events or one's past actions (or inaction), thoughts, or desires.
Index Terms: Feelings of guilt

title: Irritability
Description: A mood state characterised by being easily annoyed and provoked to anger, out of proportion to the circumstances.
Index Terms: Irritability

title: Leaden paralysis
Description: A feeling that one's arms or legs are as heavy as lead, associated with a form of depression that also commonly includes overeating and oversleeping.
Index Terms: Leaden paralysis

title: Mental rumination
Description: Mental preoccupation with negative events, personal characteristics, or failures.
Index Terms: Mental rumination

title: Restlessness
Description: A feeling of being unable to keep still.
Index Terms: Restlessness; agitation; restlessness state; Jitteriness; feeling jittery

title: Tantrum
Description: An emotional outburst, usually among children or those in emotional distress, that is typically characterised by stubbornness, crying, screaming, defiance, anger, a resistance to attempts at pacification, and in some cases hitting or other violent behaviour.
Index Terms: Tantrum

title: Worry
Description: Unpleasant thoughts that are difficult to control, related to anticipated potential negative events.
Index Terms: Worry; worries NOS

title: Symptoms or signs involving form of thought
Description: Symptoms and signs involving the logical sequence and coherence of thought, typically manifest in speech, including thought disorder (circumstantiality, tangentiality, disorganised thinking and incoherence), flight of ideas, neologisms, and thought blocking.

title: Symptoms or signs of thought disorder
Description: Disturbances in the associative thought process typically manifest in speech or writing that range from circumstantiality to incoherence. These may be indicative of Schizophrenia and other primary psychotic disorders but can also occur in other mental disorders (e.g., Delirium).

title: Circumstantiality
Description: A relatively mild disturbance in the associative thought process typically manifest in speech or writing characterised by delay in getting to the point because of the interpolation of unnecessary details and irrelevant parenthetical remarks.
Index Terms: Circumstantiality; Verbosity and circumstantial detail obscuring reason for contact

title: Tangentiality
Description: A disturbance in the associative thought process typically manifest in speech in which the person tends to digress readily from the topic under discussion to other topics through associations without ever returning to the original topic.
Index Terms: Tangentiality

title: Disorganised thinking
Description: A disturbance in the associative thought process typically manifested in speech in which the person shifts suddenly from one topic to another that is unrelated or minimally related to the first. The individual gives no indication of being aware of the disconnectedness or illogicality of their thinking.
Index Terms: Disorganised thinking; disorganised speech; loose associations; thought derailment

title: Incoherence
Description: Speech or thinking that is so disorganised that it is essentially incomprehensible to others.
Index Terms: Incoherence

title: Flight of ideas
Description: A nearly continuous flow of thoughts, usually manifested in speech, with rapid changes from topic to topic that are often based on understandable associations, distracting stimuli, or plays on words. In severe cases, the changes may be so rapid that speech is disorganised and incoherent.
Index Terms: Flight of ideas

title: Neologisms
Description: The invention of new words that have meaning only to the person using them. May also include the use of existing words in ways that are inconsistent with their common meaning.
Index Terms: Neologisms

title: Thought blocking
Description: A phenomenon usually manifested by the person's speech being suddenly interrupted by silences, experienced as a quick and total emptying of the mind.
Index Terms: Thought blocking

title: Symptoms or signs involving content of thought
Description: Symptoms and signs involving content of thought include delusions, experiences of influence, passivity, and control, grandiosity, homicidal ideation, identity disturbance, obsessions, overvalued ideas, paranoid ideation, referential thinking, suspiciousness, and suicidal ideation.

title: Delusion
Description: A belief that is demonstrably untrue or not shared by others, usually based on incorrect inference about external reality. The belief is firmly held with conviction and is not, or is only briefly, susceptible to modification by experience or evidence that contradicts it. The belief is not ordinarily accepted by other members or the person's culture or subculture (i.e., it is not an article of religious faith).

title: Bizarre delusion
Description: A delusion that involves a phenomenon that would be regarded as physically impossible within the person's cultural context.
Index Terms: Bizarre delusion

title: Delusion of being controlled
Description: A delusion that involves an external force or person controlling one's feelings, impulses, thoughts, or behaviour.
Exclusions: Experiences of influence, passivity, and control
Index Terms: Delusion of being controlled

title: Delusion of guilt
Description: A delusion involving exaggerated or inappropriate responsibility, need for punishment or retribution, or disproportionate consequences of one’s actions, such as that a minor error in the past will lead to disaster, that the person has committed a sin or horrible crime and should be punished severely, or that the person is responsible for a horrible outcome with which there can be no possible connection.
Index Terms: Delusion of guilt

title: Delusion of reference
Description: A delusion that events, objects, or other people in the person's immediate environment have a particular and unusual personal significance, usually of a negative or pejorative nature.
Index Terms: Delusion of reference

title: Erotomanic delusion
Description: A delusion that another person, usually of higher status, is in love with the individual.
Index Terms: Erotomanic delusion; Erotomania

title: Grandiose delusion
Description: A delusion of inflated worth, power, knowledge, identity, or special relationship to a deity or famous person.
Exclusions: Grandiosity
Index Terms: Grandiose delusion

title: Jealous delusion
Description: A delusion that one's sexual partner is unfaithful.
Index Terms: Jealous delusion

title: Persecutory delusion
Description: A delusion in which the central theme is that one (or someone to whom one is close) is being attacked, mocked, harassed, cheated, conspired against, or persecuted.
Index Terms: Persecutory delusion

title: Religious delusion
Description: A delusion involving religious or spiritual themes or subject matter that other members of the person's religious group do not accept as possible.
Index Terms: Religious delusion

title: Somatic delusion
Description: A delusion involving the functioning or appearance of one’s body, including of having a serious disease.
Index Terms: Somatic delusion; Somatic delusion directed at the skin; Delusion of infestation; Delusions of parasitosis; Parasitophobia

title: Nihilistic delusion
Description: A delusion that the self, part of the self, part of the body, other persons, or the whole world has ceased to exist.
Index Terms: Nihilistic delusion

title: Misidentification delusion
Description: A delusion that people in one’s environment, which may include family members and loved ones, are imposters or actors or are otherwise not who they seem to be.
Index Terms: Misidentification delusion

title: Delusion of impoverishment
Description: A delusional conviction that one is currently destitute or soon will be, or that one does not have the necessary financial resources to live on, in spite of evidence to the contrary.
Index Terms: Delusion of impoverishment

title: Experiences of influence, passivity, and control
Description: The experience that one's feelings, impulses, thoughts, bodily functions, or behaviour are under the control of another person or other external force instead of under one's own control. These experiences may or may not be accompanied by a delusional belief that provides an explanation for the subjective experience.
Exclusions: Delusion of being controlled

title: Thought broadcasting
Description: The experience that one's thoughts are accessible by others so that others know what one is thinking.
Index Terms: Thought broadcasting

title: Thought insertion
Description: The experience that certain thoughts are being placed in one's mind by others.
Index Terms: Thought insertion

title: Thought withdrawal
Description: The experience that one's thoughts are being removed by an outside person or force.
Index Terms: Thought withdrawal

title: Grandiosity
Description: Exaggerated self-esteem or an unrealistic belief in one's superiority, importance, capacities, or identity.
Exclusions: Grandiose delusion
Index Terms: Grandiosity

title: Homicidal ideation
Description: Thoughts, ideas, or ruminations about killing another person, which range from vague ideas of revenge to detailed and fully formulated plans but do not include actual homicidal attempts.
Index Terms: Homicidal ideation

title: Identity disturbance
Description: Distortion or inconsistency in the sense or view of sameness and historical continuity of one's self.
Index Terms: Identity disturbance

title: Obsessions
Description: Repetitive and persistent thoughts (e.g., of contamination), images (e.g., of violent scenes), or impulses/urges (e.g., to stab someone) that are experienced as intrusive, unwanted, and are commonly associated with anxiety.
Index Terms: Obsessions

title: Overvalued ideas
Description: Unreasonable and sustained beliefs that are maintained with less than delusional intensity (i.e., the person is able to acknowledge the possibility that the belief may not be true). An alternative use of this term is to refer to conventional or plausible thoughts (e.g., religious concepts, political ideas, or excessively idealistic beliefs) that are held with such a level of intensity so that the person's life is taken up by them.
Exclusions: Delusion; Grandiosity; Paranoid ideation; Referential thinking
Index Terms: Overvalued ideas

title: Paranoid ideation
Description: Ideation, not held with delusional intensity, involving suspiciousness or beliefs of being harassed, persecuted, or unfairly treated by others.
Exclusions: Persecutory delusion
Index Terms: Paranoid ideation

title: Referential thinking
Description: Ideation, not held with delusional intensity, that random or coincidental events are of particular and unusual significance to the person.
Exclusions: Delusion of reference
Index Terms: Referential thinking

title: Suspiciousness
Description: The behaviour of others is viewed with anxiety, mistrust, or hostility and perceived as potentially threatening.
Index Terms: Suspiciousness; Suspiciousness and marked evasiveness; suspiciousness behaviour

title: Suicidal ideation
Description: Thoughts, ideas, or ruminations about the possibility of ending one's life, ranging from thinking that one would be better off dead to formulation of elaborate plans.
Exclusions: Suicide attempt; Personal history of self-harm
Index Terms: Suicidal ideation; risk for suicide; suicidal ideation tendencies; suicidal tendencies; suicidal tendency; suicide risk

title: Symptoms or signs involving perceptual disturbance
Description: Symptoms and signs involving a disruption in sensory perception, including depersonalization, derealization, and hallucinations in any modality.
Exclusions: disturbances of skin sensation

title: Depersonalisation
Description: Experiencing the self as strange or unreal, or feeling detached from, or as though one were an outside observer of, one’s thoughts, feelings, sensations, body, or actions. Depersonalization may take the form of emotional and/or physical numbing, a sense of watching oneself from a distance or ‘being in a play’, or perceptual alterations (e.g., a distorted sense of time).
Index Terms: Depersonalisation

title: Derealisation
Description: Experiencing other persons, objects, or the world as strange or unreal (e.g., dreamlike, distant, foggy, lifeless, colourless, or visually distorted) or feeling detached from one’s surroundings.
Index Terms: Derealisation

title: Hallucinations
Description: Sensory perceptions of any modality occurring in the absence of the appropriate (external) stimulus. The person may or may not have insight into the unreal nature of the perception.

title: Auditory hallucinations
Description: Hallucinations involving the perception of sound, most frequently of voices but sometimes of clicks or other noises, that are not restricted to the period of awakening or the onset of sleep.
Index Terms: Auditory hallucinations

title: Gustatory hallucinations
Description: Hallucinations of taste in the absence of an actual external stimulus.
Index Terms: Gustatory hallucinations

title: Hypnopompic hallucinations
Description: Hallucinations that occur during the period of awakening, most commonly of the visual, tactile or auditory modality.
Index Terms: Hypnopompic hallucinations

title: Hypnagogic hallucinations
Description: Hallucinations that occur at the onset of sleep, most commonly of the visual, tactile or auditory modality.
Index Terms: Hypnagogic hallucinations

title: Olfactory hallucinations
Description: Hallucinations involving the perception of odour (e.g., of burning rubber, decaying fish, orange peel) in the absence of an actual external stimulus.
Index Terms: Olfactory hallucinations

title: Somatic hallucinations
Description: Hallucinations involving the perception of an unusual physical state or event within the body, such as an electrical impulse running down one's arms or an object inside one's chest.
Index Terms: Somatic hallucinations

title: Tactile hallucinations
Description: Hallucinations involving the perception of being touched (e.g., feeling like bugs are crawling on the skin, pins being stuck into one's finger) that are not restricted to the period of awakening or the onset of sleep.
Index Terms: Tactile hallucinations; Formication

title: Visual hallucinations
Description: Hallucinations involving sight in the absence of an actual visual stimulus that are not restricted to the period of awakening or the onset of sleep. Visual hallucinations may involve formed images, such as of people, or of unformed images, such as flashes of light. Visual hallucinations must be distinguished from illusions, which are visual misperceptions of real external stimuli.
Index Terms: Visual hallucinations; seeing things

title: Disturbance of body image
Description: Excessively negative, distorted, or inaccurate perception of one's own body or parts of it.
Index Terms: Disturbance of body image

title: Illusions
Description: A misinterpretation of a true sensation (e.g., hearing voices in the sound of running water, the perception of figures in shadows).
Exclusions: Visual illusions
Index Terms: Illusions

title: Symptoms or signs related to personality features
Description: Symptoms and signs involving the characteristics or qualities possessed by a person that uniquely influence his or her cognition, motivations, and behaviours in various situations.

title: Attention seeking
Description: A tendency to engage in behaviour designed to attract notice and to make oneself the focus of others’ attention.
Index Terms: Attention seeking

title: Callousness
Description: Lack of concern for the feelings or problems of others; a lack of guilt or remorse about the negative or harmful effects of one's actions on others.
Index Terms: Callousness

title: Eccentricity
Description: A tendency toward appearance or behaviour that is odd, unusual, peculiar, or unconventional, and is inconsistent with cultural or subcultural norms.
Index Terms: Eccentricity

title: Entitlement
Description: The belief that one is inherently deserving of privileges or special treatment.
Index Terms: Entitlement

title: Hostility
Description: A tendency to experience persistent or frequent angry feelings, especially in response to minor slights and insults, and to adopt an unfriendly or threatening attitude in interactions with others.
Index Terms: Hostility; hostile behaviour

title: Impulsivity
Description: A tendency to act on the spur of the moment in response to immediate stimuli, characterised by lack of deliberation and failure to consider risks and consequences before acting. Impulsivity may reflect a desire for immediate rewards or an inability to delay gratification.
Index Terms: Impulsivity

title: Indecisiveness
Description: A tendency to have difficulty making decisions or committing to a course of action.
Index Terms: Indecisiveness

title: Irresponsibility
Description: A pattern of disregard for and failure to honour obligations or commitments; a lack of respect for and follow-through on agreements or promises; carelessness with others' property.
Index Terms: Irresponsibility

title: Low frustration tolerance
Description: Diminished ability to regulate one's emotions and behaviour in response to frustrating circumstances.
Index Terms: Low frustration tolerance

title: Low self-esteem
Description: Low appraisal of one's self-worth.
Index Terms: Low self-esteem

title: Negative affectivity
Description: A tendency to experience a broad range of distressing emotions, e.g. anxiety, anger irritability, depression, and other negative emotional states, often in response to even relatively minor actual or perceived stressors.
Inclusions: negative emotionality; proneness to negative emotional states
Index Terms: Negative affectivity; negative emotionality; neuroticism; proneness to negative emotional states

title: Negativism
Description: A tendency to oppose or resist suggestions or advice, or to resist stubbornly for no apparent reason.
Index Terms: Negativism

title: Perfectionism
Description: An inclination to demand flawlessness of oneself or others and setting excessively high standards.
Index Terms: Perfectionism

title: Pessimism
Description: An inclination to emphasize adverse aspects, conditions, and possibilities, or to expect the worst possible outcome.
Index Terms: Pessimism

title: Recklessness
Description: A tendency to engage in behaviour that potentially endangers a person's physical health, safety, or life.
Index Terms: Recklessness

title: Sensation seeking
Description: An inclination to search for experiences and feelings that are varied, novel, complex, and intense.
Index Terms: Sensation seeking

title: Stubbornness
Description: A steadfast adherence to an opinion, purpose, or course of action in spite of reason, arguments, or persuasion.
Index Terms: Stubbornness

title: Submissiveness
Description: A tendency to adapt one’s behaviour to the actual or perceived interests and desires of others even when doing so is antithetical to one’s own interests, needs, or desires.
Index Terms: Submissiveness

title: Symptoms or signs involving eating and related behaviour
Description: Symptoms and signs related to disturbances in the regulation or form of eating behaviour that are not developmentally appropriate or culturally sanctioned, including avoidant or restrictive eating, binge eating, decreased appetite, eating of non-nutritive substances, increased appetite, purging behaviour, and rumination-regurgitation.

title: Avoidant or restrictive eating
Description: Acceptance of only a limited diet, which may be defined in terms of a specific dietary composition or sensory features of food, that is inconsistent with cultural or subcultural norms.
Exclusions: Avoidant-restrictive food intake disorder
Index Terms: Avoidant or restrictive eating

title: Binge eating
Description: An episode in which an individual eats notably more than usual and feels that she or he is unable to stop or limit the amount or type of food eaten.
Exclusions: Bulimia Nervosa; Binge eating disorder
Index Terms: Binge eating

title: Eating of non-nutritive substances
Description: Consumption of non-food objects and materials (e.g., clay, soil, chalk, plaster, plastic, metal and paper) or raw food ingredients (e.g., large quantities of salt or corn flour).
Exclusions: Pica
Index Terms: Eating of non-nutritive substances

title: Purging behaviour
Description: Behaviour aimed at the removal of ingested food from the body with the specific intention to lose weight or prevent weight gain (e.g., self-induced vomiting, laxative abuse, or the use of enemas).
Exclusions: Bulimia Nervosa; Anorexia Nervosa
Index Terms: Purging behaviour

title: Rumination-regurgitation
Description: Rechewing of previously swallowed food that has been brought back to the mouth through regurgitation, which may then be reswallowed or spat out.
Exclusions: Rumination-regurgitation disorder; Bulimia Nervosa; Anorexia Nervosa
Index Terms: Rumination-regurgitation

title: Symptoms or signs involving elimination
Description: Symptoms and signs involving the behavioural components of defecation (soiling, faecal elimination) and urination.

title: Soiling
Description: The passage of faeces in clothing, bed, or other inappropriate places in an individual who has reached a developmental age where faecal continence is ordinarily expected.
Exclusions: Encopresis
Index Terms: Soiling

title: Wetting
Description: The voiding of urine into clothes or bed, which may occur during the day or night in an individual who has reached a developmental age where urinary continence is ordinarily expected.
Exclusions: Enuresis
Index Terms: Wetting

title: Asomatognosia
Index Terms: Asomatognosia

title: Allodynia
Description: Pain due to a normally non-painful stimulus
Index Terms: Allodynia

title: Anacusis
Index Terms: Anacusis

title: Anaesthesia of skin
Description: Partial or complete loss of sensation affecting the skin, most commonly affecting a circumscribed area and resulting from sensory nerve damage as from injury or leprosy.
Index Terms: Anaesthesia of skin; Hypoaesthesia of skin; Loss of cutaneous sensation; Loss of sensation; Numbness of skin; Paraesthesia of skin; Pins and needles; Hemianaesthesia; Hemihypalgesia; Hemihypesthesia; Hemiparaesthesia; Hypoaesthesia; hypaesthesia

title: Tingling fingers or feet or toes
Index Terms: Tingling fingers or feet or toes

title: Hyperaesthesia
Description: Increased sensibility to stimuli of sense
Index Terms: Hyperaesthesia; heightened sensation; heightened sensation of skin

title: Dysesthesia
Index Terms: Dysesthesia

title: Acroparaesthesia
Description: Severe pain in the extremities
Index Terms: Acroparaesthesia; Acroparaesthesia, simple; Acroparaesthesia, vasomotor

title: Analgesia
Index Terms: Analgesia; Hemianalgesia

title: Neurological neglect syndrome
Index Terms: Neurological neglect syndrome; hemi-inattention; hemiakinesia; hemispatial neglect; left sided neglect; sensory extinction; unilateral neglect; unilateral visual inattention; visuospatial neglect

title: Disturbances of smell or taste
Description: Disturbances of smell or taste include anosmia, parosmia, parageusia, and other disturbances of smell or taste.

title: Anosmia
Index Terms: Anosmia; absent smell; complete loss of smell; loss of smell; lost sense of smell; no sense of smell; sense of smell absent

title: Parosmia
Index Terms: Parosmia; Disturbance of olfactory nerve; distorted sense of smell; disturbance of smell; hyperosmia; loss of sense of smell; olfactory alteration; perversion of the sense of smell; sense of smell altered; smell disorder

title: Dysgeusia
Description: A disorder characterised by an alteration of the sense of taste
Index Terms: Dysgeusia; adverse taste perception; ageusia; cacogeusia; disorder of taste; disturbance of taste; gustatory alteration; loss of taste sense; parageusia; perversion of sense of taste; taste sense altered; things taste abnormal; things taste different; things taste peculiar

title: Hyposmia
Description: Decreased ability to smell
Index Terms: Hyposmia

title: Phonophobia
Description: Hypersensitivity to sounds
Index Terms: Phonophobia

title: Dyssomnia
Description: Difficulties to fall asleep, or to remain sleeping
Index Terms: Dyssomnia

title: Abnormalities of gait or mobility
Description: Abnormalities of gait or mobility include ataxic gait, paralytic gait, difficulty in walking, immobility, and other abnormalities of gait or mobility.
Exclusions: Ataxia, unspecified; Hereditary ataxia; ataxia, locomotor (syphilitic); Immobility syndrome

title: Ataxic gait
Index Terms: Ataxic gait; Staggering gait; gait ataxia

title: Paralytic gait
Description: A collection of gait abnormalities due to affected motor control, sensory feedback, and muscle strength.
Index Terms: Paralytic gait; Spastic gait; paralysis of gait; rigid gait; spasm of gait; spastic paraplegic gait

title: Difficulty in walking
Index Terms: Difficulty in walking; difficult walking; dysbasia

title: Immobility
Exclusions: Catatonia; Psychomotor retardation
Index Terms: Immobility; Bedfast; Chairfast; bed bound; bed ridden; inactivity NOS; nonambulatory

title: Lack of coordination
Description: Other lack of coordination is a lack of coordination other than abnormal involuntary movements and abnormalities of gait and mobility.
Exclusions: Ataxic gait; Hereditary ataxia; Vertigo

title: Ataxia, unspecified
Index Terms: Ataxia, unspecified; ataxia NOS; ataxic; ataxy; dyssynergia; general ataxia NOS; nonorganic ataxia

title: Automatism
Description: Repetitive unconscious gestures such as lip smacking, chewing or swallowing
Index Terms: Automatism

title: Atonia
Description: Loss of muscle tone
Index Terms: Atonia

title: Head drop
Index Terms: Head drop

title: Intention tremor
Description: Cerebellar tremor characterised by a broad course and low frequency
Index Terms: Intention tremor

title: Abnormal involuntary movements
Description: Abnormal involuntary movements include abnormal head movements, tremor, cramp, spasm, fasciculation, and other abnormal involuntary movements
Exclusions: Movement disorders; Stereotyped movement disorder; Tic disorders; Essential tremor or related tremors; Intention tremor

title: Asterixis
Index Terms: Asterixis

title: Abnormal head movements
Index Terms: Abnormal head movements

title: Athetosis
Description: Twisting and writhing movements
Index Terms: Athetosis; acquired athetosis; athetoid; Unilateral athetosis; hemiathetosis

title: Drop attack
Description: A sudden spontaneous fall while standing and recovery within seconds or minutes
Index Terms: Drop attack

title: Titubation
Description: Head tremor of cerebellar origin
Index Terms: Titubation

title: Shuddering
Index Terms: Shuddering

title: Abnormal reflex
Exclusions: abnormal pupillary reflex; hyperactive gag reflex; vasovagal reaction or syncope
Index Terms: Abnormal reflex; motor disorder; motor disturbance; reflex disorder; reflex disturbance; Areflexia; Hyporeflexia; Hyperreflexia

title: Abnormal posture
Index Terms: Abnormal posture; imperfect posture; malposture; Posture imbalance; Relaxation of posture

title: Clonus
Description: A series of involuntary muscle contractions and relaxations
Index Terms: Clonus

title: Cramp or spasm
Exclusions: carpopedal spasm; Infantile spasms
Index Terms: Cramp or spasm; cramp; crampy; spasm; Facial hemispasm; Muscle cramp; muscular cramp; Muscle rigidity; Muscle spasticity; spasticity NOS; Muscle spasm

title: Dystonia
Description: Sustained muscle contraction, involuntary movements that can lead to fixed abnormal postures
Index Terms: Dystonia; dystonia disorder; Dyskinesia NOS; dyskinetic

title: Fasciculation
Index Terms: Fasciculation; flickering muscles; fluttering muscles; involuntary muscle contractions; muscle fasciculation; muscle twitching; muscular fasciculation; spontaneous contraction of muscle; twitching; twitching NOS; writhing muscles; Myokymia

title: Meningismus
Index Terms: Meningismus

title: Muscle fibrillation
Description: An involuntary muscle contraction and relaxation in a muscle fiber
Index Terms: Muscle fibrillation

title: Muscular hypertonia
Index Terms: Muscular hypertonia

title: Myotonia
Description: Slow relaxation of the muscles after voluntary contraction
Index Terms: Myotonia

title: Ophthalmoparesis
Description: Paresis of one or more extraocular muscles
Index Terms: Ophthalmoparesis

title: Opisthotonos
Description: Arching position of the body due spasm of the axial muscles along the spinal column
Index Terms: Opisthotonos

title: Tendency to fall
Inclusions: Tendency to fall because of old age or other unclear health problems
Exclusions: accidents; Difficulty in walking; Dizziness or giddiness; falls causing injury; Syncope or collapse
Index Terms: Tendency to fall; Tendency to fall because of old age or other unclear health problems; falls NOS; multiple falls NOS; recurrent falls; repeated falls; Complications of fall

title: Tetany
Exclusions: Parathyroid tetany; post-thyroidectomy tetany
Index Terms: Tetany; Carpopedal spasm; Hyperkinetic tetany; Pseudotetany

title: Dizziness or giddiness
Description: Terms which may refer to a sense of spatial disorientation, motion of the environment, or lightheadedness. Dizziness is a general feeling of being off-balance. Giddiness is the feeling that you or your surroundings are moving or spinning.
Exclusions: Vertiginous syndromes

title: Vertigo of central origin
Description: Central vertigo is usually a result of an abnormal processing of the vestibular sensory input by the central nervous system due to either a disruption of central integrators (i.e. brain stem, cerebellum) or a sensory information mismatch (i.e. from the cortex). Lesions that affect the vestibular nerve or root entry zone (i.e. cerebellopontine angle [CPA] lesions) result in imbalance by affecting primary vestibular sensory information
Index Terms: Vertigo of central origin; Central positional nystagmus; central positional vertigo; central vertigo; central vestibular vertigo; cerebellar vertigo; cerebral vertigo; vertigo of central origin, unspecified ear

title: Disorder of equilibrium
Index Terms: Disorder of equilibrium

title: Exertional dizziness
Index Terms: Exertional dizziness

title: Light-headedness
Index Terms: Light-headedness; light headed

title: Presyncope

Description: This category includes the abnormalities of quantity, colour and odor in sputum which may suggest an aetiology. Patients with chronic bronchitis typically expectorate small quantities of mucoid yellow material. title: Sleep related Cheyne-Stokes respiration
Description: Periodic breathing, a variant of Cheyne-Stokes respiration, is characterised by regular, recurrent cycles of changing tidal volumes in which the lowest tidal volume is less than half the maximal tidal volume in that cycle. It is the most frequent abnormal respiratory pattern directly related to stroke rather than underlying systemic disease, occurring in approximately 25 percent of patients. Periodic breathing may be more common among patients with subarachnoid haemorrhage.
Exclusions: Central sleep apnea with Cheyne-Stokes breathing
Index Terms: Sleep related Cheyne-Stokes respiration; Sleep related Cheyne-Stokes breathing

title: Dyspnoea
Description:
Dyspnoea is used to describe perceptions of difficulty or distress related to breathing and is recognised as symptomatic of disease when it occurs under inappropriate circumstances. Dyspnoea is a presenting complaint of patients with a wide variety of medical diseases by multiple mechanisms. 
Dyspnoea is considered acute when it lasts from hours up to 3 weeks, subacute from 3 weeks up to 8 weeks, and chronic dyspnoea lasts more than 8 weeks.
Exclusions: Transient tachypnoea of newborn

title: Hiccough
Description: Hiccough are repeated involuntary spasms of the diaphragm followed by sudden closure of the glottis, which checks the inflow of air and causes the characteristic sound. Transient episodes are very common. Persistent (> 2 days) and intractable (> 1 mo) Hiccough are uncommon but quite distressing.
Index Terms: Hiccough; finding of hiccoughs; hiccup; observation of hiccoughs; singultus; spasm of diaphragm

title: Hyperventilation
Description: Hyperventilation refers to an increase in the rate of alveolar ventilation that is excessive for the rate of metabolic carbon dioxide production, resulting in a decrease in arterial PCO2 to below the normal range of 37 to 43 mm Hg. Hyperventilation should be distinguished from tachypnoea, an increase in respiratory frequency, and from hyperpnea, an increase in minute volume of ventilation.
Index Terms: Hyperventilation; HV - [hyperventilation]; hyperpnoea; hyperpnoea tetany; hyperventilating; hyperventilation tetany; increased respiratory rate; laboured breathing; overbreathing

title: Mouth breathing
Description: Breathing through mouth. Nasal obstruction may also necessitate mouth breathing, which itself can precipitate obstructive apnoea. Breathing through the mouth may also increase risk for OSA [obstructive sleep apnoea] by its effect on the tongue. The tongue forms the anterior wall of the oropharynx; both the supine posture and opening of the mouth tend to displace it posteriorly and encourage airway closure.
Exclusions: Dry mouth

title: Stertor
Description: Stertor is a heavy snoring or gasping sound on inspiration occurring in coma or deep sleep that may be caused by partial obstruction of airway, choanal stenosis, enlarged tonsils and/or adenoids, and redundant upper airway tissues.

title: Sneezing
Description: Sneezing is one of the most fundamental airway reflexes, which is characterised by a deep preparatory inspiration followed by an abrupt increase in subglottic pressure reflecting a forceful, active expiration rather similar to coughing. Chemical or physical stimuli to the nasal mucosa may initiate potent respiratory and cardiovascular reflexes via stimulation of trigeminal nerves. Mild stimuli result in sneezing and nasal hypersecretion. These reflex responses protect the lower airways from inhalation of physical and chemical irritants.
Index Terms: Sneezing; sneeze; sneezing symptom

title: Stridor

title: Pain in throat or chest
Description:
Pain in throat or chest means having pain sensation in throat or chest. Throat pain is a common ailment characterized by scratchiness, or irritation in the throat. It is frequently a symptom of an infection, but it can be due to many other conditions.
Chest pain is any sort of pain felt in your chest area — from your shoulders to the bottom of your ribs.
Chest pain can be a symptom of many different conditions, some of which are more serious than others.

title: Chest pain on breathing
Description: Pleuritic chest pain is a type of pain that is caused by problems with the thin layers of tissue that surround the lungs (called the “pleura”). This type of pain feels like a sharp, stabbing chest pain, and it gets worse when you breathe in. Pleuritic chest pain can be caused by the following problems: pneumothorax, pleural effusion, pleuritis, empyema, pericarditis.

title: Abdominal compartment syndrome
Description: Abdominal compartment syndrome is a condition of organ dysfunction caused by increased intra-abdominal pressure (intra-abdominal hypertension), possibly due to intra-abdominal haemorrhage, retroperitoneal haematoma, or intestinal oedema, often occurred after surgical intervention or trauma, or often associated with septic condition. The importance of this clinical entity was recognised recently in the end of the 20th Century. Usually the abdominal distension due to primary ischaemic bowel injury was excluded from this clinical entity.
Index Terms: Abdominal compartment syndrome

title: Ascites
Description: Accumulation or retention of free fluid in the abdominal peritoneal cavity between the tissues lining the abdomen and abdominal organs. The fluid may be serous, haemorrhagic, or the result of inflammation or tumour metastasis to the peritoneum.

title: Constipation
Description: Constipation is an acute or chronic condition in which bowel movements occur less often than usual or consist of hard, dry stools that are often painful or difficult to pass. title: Perforation of small intestine
Description: Small intestinal perforation is a complete penetration of the wall of small intestine, often resulting in the leakage of small intestinal contents into the abdominal cavity.

title: Obscure gastrointestinal bleeding
Description: Obscure gastrointestinal bleeding (OGIB) is defined as gastrointestinal bleeding with no source identified at upper and lower endoscopy. Despite a thorough endoscopic examination, the origin of the blood loss remains unexplained and observed for further bleeding.

title: Haematochezia
Description: Haematochezia is the passage of fresh blood through the anus, usually in or with stools (contrast with melena). Haematochezia is commonly associated with lower gastrointestinal bleeding.
Index Terms: Haematochezia

title: Melaena
Description: It is bloody stools that indicate bleeding from vascular system in the digestive tract. It is also described as black, tarry, and foul-smelling stools or red/maroon-coloured stools that contain degraded blood.

title: Haematemesis
Description: Vomiting of blood that is either fresh bright red, or older "coffee-ground" in character. Vomiting blood is a regurgitation of blood through the upper gastrointestinal tract and it generally indicates bleeding of the upper gastrointestinal tract.
Index Terms: Haematemesis; blood in vomit; vomiting blood

title: Positive occult blood in stool
Description: Positive tests (positive stool) determined by faecal occult blood testing (FOBT), which aims to detect subtle blood loss in the gastrointestinal tract. Positive occult blood in stool may suggest gastrointestinal bleeding and warrant further investigation, especially for malignancy.
Index Terms: Positive occult blood in stool; FOBT - [faecal occult blood testing]

title: Skin lesion of uncertain or unspecified nature
Description: To be used where there is either significant uncertainty or alternatively no information as to the nature of a circumscribed skin lesion. This is of particular importance with regard to whether or not the lesion may be malignant.

title: Skin lesion of uncertain nature
Description: This denotes the presence of a skin lesion but uncertainty as to its nature. No inference as to whether the lesion might be of serious significance (e.g. suspected skin cancer) is made.

title: Acute skin eruption of uncertain or unspecified nature
Description: A provisional diagnosis for an acute skin eruption of less than six weeks' duration of unknown, uncertain or unspecified nature.
Exclusions: Drug eruptions
Index Terms: Acute skin eruption of uncertain or unspecified nature; Acute pustular skin eruption; Acute rash; Acute skin eruption without established diagnosis

title: Acute erythematous skin eruption
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists predominantly of diffuse cutaneous erythema. A classical cause is scarlet fever but reactions to other bacterial toxins, drugs and certain foods or acute graft-versus-host disease may present with a similar picture.
Index Terms: Acute erythematous skin eruption

title: Acute purpuric skin eruption
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists predominantly of disseminated purpura. Potential causes are numerous and include thrombocytopenia, coagulopathies, vasculitides and sepsis.
Index Terms: Acute purpuric skin eruption; Acute haemorrhagic skin eruption; Acute petechial skin eruption

title: Acute urticarial skin eruption
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists of urticaria-like papules and plaques. Drugs are a common precipitant.
Index Terms: Acute urticarial skin eruption; Acute urticarial dermatitis

title: Acute maculopapular skin eruption
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists of multiple macules and papules. Viral infections and drugs are common precipitants.
Index Terms: Acute maculopapular skin eruption

title: Acute papular skin eruption
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists of multiple skin papules. A wide variety of infectious and inflammatory skin disorders may present in this way. Examples include guttate psoriasis, lichen planus, pityriasis lichenoides, insect bites, scabies and secondary syphilis.
Index Terms: Acute papular skin eruption

title: Acute exudative skin eruption
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and in which exudation and crusting are prominent features. Common causes are infected eczema, acute allergic contact dermatitis and impetigo.
Index Terms: Acute exudative skin eruption

title: Acute blistering skin eruption
Description: A provisional diagnosis for an acute blistering skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature. Examples include vesicular dermatitis of the hands and feet (pompholyx), acute phototoxic reactions, especially to contact with plants (phytophotodermatitis), sunburn and immunobullous disorders such as bullous pemphigoid.
Index Terms: Acute blistering skin eruption

title: Acute desquamating skin eruption
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and in which desquamation (shedding of skin scales) is a prominent feature. This is seen characteristically in the later stages of many acute viral exanthemata but may also be seen in drug reactions and in unstable and erythrodermic psoriasis.
Index Terms: Acute desquamating skin eruption

title: Acute discoid or annular skin eruption
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists of multiple circular or ring-shaped patches and plaques. Although infection due to dermatophyte infection (tinea or ringworm) can produce this pattern, many other skin disorders may have a discoid or annular configuration: these are frequently misdiagnosed as tinea. Common examples include atopic eczema, nummular dermatitis and psoriasis.
Index Terms: Acute discoid or annular skin eruption

title: Acute excoriation of skin
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a skin eruption of unknown or uncertain nature which arises abruptly and consists of multiple excoriations. In the majority of cases the excoriation is secondary to intense pruritus arising either from an underlying systemic disorder such as cholestatic jaundice or from a pruritic skin disease such as eczema. In some cases, however, psychogenic factors may be responsible.
Index Terms: Acute excoriation of skin

title: Chronic skin disorder of uncertain or unspecified nature
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder (of at least six weeks' duration) of unknown, uncertain or unspecified nature.
Index Terms: Chronic skin disorder of uncertain or unspecified nature; Chronic rash; Chronic skin disorder without established diagnosis

title: Chronic erythematous skin disorder
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder of unknown or uncertain nature in which widespread confluent erythema is the predominant feature. Examples include generalised atopic eczema, erythrodermic psoriasis, pityriasis rubra pilaris and Sézary syndrome.
Index Terms: Chronic erythematous skin disorder

title: Chronic urticarial skin disorder
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a rash consisting of persistent urticated papules and plaques for which a more precise diagnosis has not been or cannot be made. Diagnoses which should be considered include immunobullous disorders, urticarial vasculitis and drug reactions.
Index Terms: Chronic urticarial skin disorder; Chronic dermal hypersensitivity reaction; Chronic urticarial dermatitis

title: Chronic papular skin disorder
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder of unknown, uncertain or unspecified nature and characterised by the presence of multiple skin papules.
Index Terms: Chronic papular skin disorder

title: Chronic blistering skin disorder
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic blistering skin disorder of unknown or uncertain nature. Examples include chronic vesicular dermatitis of the hands and feet, epidermolysis bullosa and immunobullous disorders such as bullous pemphigoid.
Index Terms: Chronic blistering skin disorder; Unspecified bullous dermatosis

title: Chronic scaling or hyperkeratotic skin disorder
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder of unknown or uncertain nature in which scaling and hyperkeratosis are prominent features. Examples include psoriasis, ichthyoses, small plaque parapsoriasis and mycosis fungoides.
Index Terms: Chronic scaling or hyperkeratotic skin disorder

title: Chronic lichenified skin disorder
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder of unknown or uncertain nature in which lichenification is the prominent feature. Examples include chronic eczema, lichen simplex and lichen planus.
Index Terms: Chronic lichenified skin disorder

title: Chronic discoid or annular skin disorder
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder of unknown or uncertain nature consisting of multiple circular or ring-shaped patches and plaques. Although infection due to dermatophyte infection (tinea or ringworm) can produce this pattern, many other skin disorders may have a discoid or annular configuration: these are frequently misdiagnosed as tinea. Common examples include atopic eczema, nummular dermatitis and psoriasis.
Index Terms: Chronic discoid or annular skin disorder

title: Chronic excoriation of skin
Description: A provisional diagnosis for a chronic skin disorder of unknown or uncertain nature characterised by the presence of multiple excoriations. In some cases the excoriation is secondary to intense pruritus arising either from an underlying systemic disorder such as uraemia or from a pruritic skin disease such as eczema. In many cases, however, psychogenic factors may be responsible.
Index Terms: Chronic excoriation of skin

title: Non-specific cutaneous vascular signs
Description: Changes perceptible in the skin as a result of alterations in blood composition, blood flow or blood vessel integrity. They may be due to local factors or may indicate underlying disorders such as anaemia, hypovolaemia, fever, hypoxia or defective clotting.
Index Terms: Non-specific cutaneous vascular signs

title: Erythema
Description: Redness of skin due to the presence of increased amounts of oxygenated haemoglobin within dilated skin capillaries. It may be due to localised or generalised inflammatory processes but may result from increased cutaneous blood flow following exertion or associated with pyrexia.
Index Terms: Erythema

title: Cyanosis
Description: A blue-purple discolouration of the skin due to the presence of increased amounts of deoxygenated blood in skin blood vessels.
Exclusions: acrocyanosis; cyanotic attacks of newborn
Index Terms: Cyanosis; cyanotic NOS; cyanotic attack NOS

title: Pallor
Description: Paleness of the skin such as may ensue from severe anaemia or from hypovolaemic shock.
Index Terms: Pallor

title: Petechiae
Description: Petechiae result from focal leakage of blood from dermal capillaries into the adjacent dermal connective tissue. They present as multiple pin-point non-blanching red or purple macules. The many underlying causes range from the innocuous (e.g. coughing or straining) to life-threatening conditions (e.g. meningococcal septicaemia).
Index Terms: Petechiae; Petechial haemorrhage; blood spots on skin; cutaneous haemorrhage; subcutaneous haemorrhage

title: Flushing
Description: Paroxysmal vasodilatation of skin capillaries.
Index Terms: Flushing

title: Disturbances of skin sensation of unspecified aetiology
Description: A group of cutaneous symptoms for which it is frequently impossible to identify a precise cause.
Index Terms: Disturbances of skin sensation of unspecified aetiology

title: Burning of skin
Description: A burning sensation in the skin which usually arises without obvious explanation.
Index Terms: Burning of skin; Burning sensation; Disturbance in temperature sense

title: Itching of skin
Description: The sensation of itch in the skin. For persistent itch of unknown cause the term "Pruritus of unknown cause" should be used.
Index Terms: Itching of skin; Pruritus of unspecified aetiology

title: Pain or tenderness of skin
Exclusions: Chronic pain
Index Terms: Pain or tenderness of skin; Hyperpathia

title: Stinging of skin
Description: An unpleasant sensation such as may be provoked by stinging nettles but which can be set off in some individuals by a wide variety of topical preparations or stimuli which are otherwise well tolerated by most people.
Index Terms: Stinging of skin

title: Tingling of skin
Description: A prickling sensation in the skin which may result from external factors such as rain falling on the skin or may be due to transient or permanent peripheral nerve damage.
Index Terms: Tingling of skin

title: Miscellaneous non-specific skin-related symptoms or signs
Description: Other specified skin changes which cannot be more precisely defined.
Index Terms: Miscellaneous non-specific skin-related symptoms or signs

title: Abnormal sensitivity to light or UV radiation of uncertain or unspecified nature

title: Changes in skin texture
Description: Alterations in skin texture of unspecified cause.
Index Terms: Changes in skin texture; Skin textural disturbance; Thickening of skin; Skin sclerosis; induration of skin; Skin crusting; Skin scaling

title: Excess and redundant skin
Description: A condition which typically occurs in formerly grossly obese individuals following massive weight loss, as following bariatric surgery or severe calorie restriction.
Index Terms: Excess and redundant skin; Flabbiness of skin; Localised excess and redundant skin; Generalised excess and redundant skin; Generalised excess and redundant skin due to intentional calorie restriction; Generalised excess and redundant skin following bariatric surgery; Generalised excess and redundant skin due to disease or malnutrition

title: Symptom or complaint relating to hair or scalp

title: Symptom or complaint relating to nails

title: Complaint of abnormal sweating
Description: Complaint that sweating is abnormal (most commonly that it is increased) without sufficient evidence to make a specific diagnosis.
Index Terms: Complaint of abnormal sweating

title: Rash
Description: A non-specific term indicating the presence of an acquired skin disturbance to be used only when no more precise information is available.
Exclusions: Acute skin eruption of uncertain or unspecified nature; Chronic skin disorder of uncertain or unspecified nature

title: Rash localised
Description: A very non-specific term to denote a localised acquired visible alteration of the skin from normal in situations where a more precise description or diagnosis cannot be made. If a diagnosis cannot be made then a choice from the classes Acute skin eruption of uncertain or unspecified nature and Chronic skin disorder of uncertain or unspecified nature is preferred.
Exclusions: Drug eruptions
Index Terms: Rash localised

title: Rash generalised
Description: A very non-specific term to denote a widespread acquired visible alteration of the skin from normal in situations where a more precise description or diagnosis cannot be made. If a diagnosis cannot be made then a choice from the classes Acute skin eruption of uncertain or unspecified nature and Chronic skin disorder of uncertain or unspecified nature is preferred.
Exclusions: Drug eruptions
Index Terms: Rash generalised

title: Cervical spine pain
Description: This is a condition which is usually characterised by pain or discomfort in the neck region and can be caused by numerous spinal problems. It may be a feature of virtually every disorder and disease that occurs above the shoulder blades.

title: Thoracic spine pain

Exclusions: Chronic primary thoracic pain; Chronic secondary musculoskeletal pain; Chronic neuropathic pain

title: Retrograde ejaculation
Description: Retrograde Ejaculation is a condition in which semen that is normally ejaculated via the urethra is redirected to the urinary bladder. Retrograde Ejaculation is typically accompanied by subjective orgasm, though the man may notice that release of semen is limited or absent. Retrograde Ejaculation most commonly occurs as a complication of transurethral prostatic resection, but may also be caused by other surgery of the pelvic area, nervous system dysfunction, or use of pharmacological agents. Confirmation is by identification of spermatozoa in a urine sample.
Index Terms: Retrograde ejaculation

title: Retractile testis migrans
Description: A retractile testicle is one that may move back and forth between the scrotum and the groin. For most boys, the problem resolves sometime before or during puberty.

title: Diffuse mesangial sclerosis
Description: Diffuse mesangial sclerosis is a histological appearance which is characterised by diffuse thickening of basement membrane and massive enlargement of mesangial areas leading to contraction and sclerosis of the glomerular capillary tuft. It may be seen in children with early onset steroid resistant nephrotic syndrome due to a variety of genetic abnormalities, either as an isolated renal disease or as part of a multi-organ syndrome.
Index Terms: Diffuse mesangial sclerosis

title: Fibronectin glomerulopathy
Description: Fibronectin glomerulopathy is a rare hereditary kidney disease in which fibronectin (FN1) deposits are seen in the mesangium and subendothelial space. The clinical picture is characterised by proteinuria, type IV renal tubular acidosis, microscopic haematuria and hypertension that may lead to end-stage renal failure in the second to sixth decade of life. This disease may be associated with mutations in the FN1 gene.
Index Terms: Fibronectin glomerulopathy

title: Lipoprotein glomerulopathy
Description: Characteristic lipoprotein thrombi are found in the glomerulus in this genetically determined disease mainly found in East Asia.
Index Terms: Lipoprotein glomerulopathy

title: Diabetic glomerular changes
Description: Diabetic glomerulosclerosis involves diffuse thickening of the basement membrane progressing to diffuse mesangial expansion (diffuse diabetic glomerulosclerosis) with in some cases matrix occupying the capillary lumen to form Kimmelstiel Wilson nodules (nodular glomerulosclerosis)
Index Terms: Diabetic glomerular changes; Glomerular disorders in diabetes mellitus; diabetic glomerulopathy

title: Pauci-immune proliferative glomerulonephritis
Description: A focal and segmental necrotising glomerulonephritis with no immune deposits (“pauci-immune”). Typical of glomerular involvement in anti-neutrophil cytoplasmic antibody (ANCA) mediated vasculitis – microscopic polyangiitis and Wegener's granulomatosis. Most but not all patients have circulation ANCA when there is active disease.
Index Terms: Pauci-immune proliferative glomerulonephritis

title: Anti-glomerular basement membrane antibody mediated disease
Description: Anti-GBM mediated glomerulonephritis is an aggressive focal and segmental proliferative glomerular disease characterised by linear staining of the glomerular basement membrane for immunoglobulins, particularly IgG and IgM. Crescentic change is often associated and circulating anti-bodies to glomerular basement membrane are found in active disease. The renal syndrome is often acute nephritis with rapid renal functional decline (rapidly progressive nephritis) and if associated with respiratory involvement (haemoptysis, respiratory failure) the couple is termed “Goodpasture's syndrome”.
Index Terms: Anti-glomerular basement membrane antibody mediated disease; Anti-glomerular basement membrane antibody disease; Goodpasture syndrome

Description: The presence of free haemoglobin in the urine, indicating haemolysis of erythrocytes within the vascular system. After saturating the haemoglobin-binding proteins (haptoglobins), free haemoglobin begins to appear in the urine.

title: Myoglobinuria
Description: Myoglobinuria is the presence of myoglobin in the urine usually as result of rhabdomyolysis. Any process that interferes with the storage or use of energy by muscle cells can lead to myoglobinuria. When excreted into the urine, myoglobin can precipitate, causing tubular obstruction and acute kidney injury. The most common causes of myoglobinuria in adults are trauma, alcohol and drug abuse, usually in relation to muscle necrosis from prolonged immobilization and pressure by the body weight. Prolonged ethanol consumption and seizure activity, similar to excessive physical activity, can produce an imbalance between muscle energy consumption and production, resulting in muscle destruction.
Index Terms: Myoglobinuria; myoglobulinuria

title: Proteinuria

title: Bence Jones proteinuria

Originally detected by precipitating at 56 and dissolving again at 100 degrees centigrade (Henry Bence Jones 1813-1873) they are now detected by urinary electrophoresis or light chain assay.
